taxonomy: Brizula nutans is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Chloridoideae, which is a group of grasses commonly known as the grama grasses or chloridoideae grasses. The genus Brizula is part of the tribe Chlorideae, a group of grasses with similar characteristics. Brizula nutans is native to South Africa, where it is found in a variety of habitats. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary history and its place within the larger group of grasses.
